entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt:
FMVs are green and very few run at full speed, but they are skippable. The first menu is also green but that goes away when you hit start. 
Gameplay looks pretty much exactly as intended, runs anywhere between 15-20 fps depending on the track and number of opponents, dips to 10 if temperatures rise or low power mode is enabled. Recommend performance testing on M series for more data.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt:
The game as a whole is stable and remains playable. The performance is acceptable: sometimes there are small FPS drawdowns and delays when loading textures, but this does not have a critical effect on gameplay.

The main problem is related to the sound.
During the intro at startup, in cut scenes, as well as when interacting with objects containing voice acting (for example, flasks with memories), audio is played with strong distortion. The sound is excessively loud, accompanied by a squeak and an overload effect, as if the volume level has been increased many times.

Additionally:
To start the game correctly, you must disable the "Force 10bpc Presenter Output” setting in the emulator. When this option is enabled, the game does not start (at least in the current configuration).

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt:
Game loads up, and runs decently with moderate slowdown and audio hiccups.

On initial run, the main menu may load weirdly, but is usually fixed in subsequent runs.

Videos work fine, but lack any form of audio, and some in-engine cutscenes may also have issues with audio. 

Visual glitches occur, especially with the lower body of the player character when standing still.

Game crashes occasionally, usually when entering new sections of the story. Intro runs decently, leading up to the first memory.

Game also crashes at a specific point in the tutorial,(screenshot attached where Ezio is looking at Altair’s ghost on the wooden brackets) 

So far, using a save from later on doesn’t seem to work well as later areas induce massive slowdowns with the increasing number of buildings and NPCS.

Slows down occasionally, lights also visible through geometry, and models sometimes appear and disappear.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt:
I’d rate performance ok, however in my opinion locked 30fps doesn’t really seem to be a strict requirement for this game to be totally playable. Ranges from about 18-25, with higher framerate especially in combat. Minor transparency rendering issues with characters hair. Other than that, nothing noticeable. 

Do note that there are severe audio issues in cutscenes and no audio in game.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt:
[Visuals]:
 - Vertical line in the middle of the screen sometimes appears for a split second.
 - Lighting bugs on either side of the screen divided by vertical line mentioned above, usually random, not specific to any spot or looking angle.
 - Buggy textures on FPS hands
[Audio/Performance]:
 - Explosions, blood splatter, particles and/or volume/smoke may cause slight audio stuttering.
 - Performance drops depending on the environment, amount of enemies and presence of smoke/fog.

[FPS Range After Loading Save]: 10-28
No crashes, managed to complete few missions.

Audio for Cutscenes, Loading Screens, Intro, Logos on boot works fine.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt: FPS hovers around 15-25fps… many stating teleporting causes crash. My game did not crash at all and in fact it seems to stabilize fps the more i play it but it doesnt exceed 24fps. It’s honestly good enough for me to feel confident playing. But i am on extremely expensive hardware. It’s a IPad Pro 13in M4 1TB 16GB Ram. This alone feels like it should be running at at 40+ fps but im going to assume new updates to the emulator will fix this over time.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt:
The intro FMV has major graphical issues, but the rest of the game seems unaffected. After shader comp: Menuing works fine, in-game it sits at around 30FPS with the game slowing down to around 50% speed. It’s not unplayable and likely performs closer to full speed on a more powerful device, the A15 Bionic isn’t great for this. 

Internal display resolution set to 640x480, though DOA5 seems unaffected by it.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt: Game has some graphical bugs during intro screen, after shader compilation, fps increases and game runs 30-57 fps.

entries.author: xenios-jp

The version of xenios that I used is 1.0.0 from CI #35: Commit d8c1019 pushed by xenios-jp in the actions section

entries.author: TheVaroX
entries.excerpt:
- The introductory videos (such as the EA intro or the Razón and Cross cinematics) have glitches and issues, but this does not affect gameplay.
- The "Campaign" works up to a certain point, where the audio cuts out and the game subsequently freezes (see last attached image).
- The "Challenge Series" mode works almost entirely (I haven't finished it completely).
- Achievement unlocking and online play have not been verified.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt:
- XeniOS is inside of LiveContainer nightly with universal.js as its script and it boots up the App Store version of StikDebug (the App Store version launches JIT for other apps just fine)
- The PiP window almost never shows up
- Halo 3 usually opens into a black screen but sometimes the entire app just closes instantly
- I have all modern scripts in the scripts folder of the App Store version of StikDebug

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt: As of the new update, Budokai 3’s Dragon Universe mode no longer shows a black screen. Enemy characters are now visible. Budokai 3 runs great. In Budokai 1, the character select screen still has texture glitches, but the game is no longer a black screen during battles.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt: In my case I had to convert the dumped game’s ISO file using extract-iso. The game itself is a launcher for each .xex file for the individual games. Each game must be manually booted by pressing the + button in XeniOS and selecting the respective folder for each title (DBZ1 for Budokai 1 and DBZ3 for Budokai 3). Both games have good performance. Budokai 1 has more graphical issues than Budokai 3. In the character select screen for Budokai 1, there are graphical glitches with the characters’ artwork. When entering a battle, a black screen appears. For Budokai 3, a black screen appears when entering Dragon Universe mode. During battles, the enemy character is invisible and only appears when using a blast attack (ex. Kamehameha), teleporting, activating Hyper Mode, or triggering Dragon Rush. Overall, both games run very well, with Budokai 3 having fewer issues.

Game loads up, intros are still not really working.

Upon moving past the intro, it will usually appear as a black screen, with the time vortex being the only thing showing, however on some instances, it attempts to render geometry, but it’s all blue.

entries.author: xenios-jp
entries.excerpt: This is a challenging 3D game that pushes the boundaries. Game Hangs or freezes a few times after starting and selecting Dark/Brightness setting, before you can get in game. Menu text is blurred and not readable making navigation challenging. The trick that seemed to allow it to load in game is navigating to Settings and manually changing the Brightness settings before trying to start. Then back out and start the game. When you first get in game it takes a few seconds for the world to load in, causing colorful or blank areas. In game the FPS is low average around 10-15 FPS. However despite these issues the game does run and is playable.

entries.author: vivat-concordia
entries.excerpt:
Field Value 
 :--- :--- 
 **Title** Lost Odyssey 
 **Title ID** `4D5307FA` 
 **Status** 🟦 In-Game 
 **Performance** Great 
 **Platform** macOS 
 **Device** Mac14,3 
 **OS Version** macOS 15.7 
 **Architecture** arm64 
 **GPU Backend** MSC 

**Notes**
As I see it, these are typical problems. Cutscenes feature a riot of green and pink colors on the screen and choppy sound, making it impossible to understand anything. Gameplay is smooth, with only occasional stutters. I'm not saying "playable" out of caution; it needs to be fully tested, which would take tens of hours. Oh, and for some reason, all games need to be launched and closed several times before they get past the initial loading screens. Then, apparently, a cache is created and the problem goes away.
